title: Question: Best practice for overriding VL_VPRINTF and VL_VPRINTF
body:
---


Author Name: **Amir Gonnen**
Original Redmine Message: 2146 from https://www.veripool.org



---

Hi Wilson,
I'm running the verilated module as part of a larger project and would like to redirect printing to our logging system.
In verilated.h, VL_VPRINTF and VL_VPRINTF are "Functions overridable by user defines". I'm looking for the best practice for doing this.
If I simply define these macros using the -D option to my functions, I'll get "identifier not found" when compiling the verilated files, since they do not include my headers (where my print functions are declared) and the compiler is not aware of the prototype of my functions.
I could, of course, insert an "#incldue my header" to verilated.h, but this would defeat purpose of "redefining" these functions, since I could as well edit verilated.h and replace the definition of VL_VPRINTF and VL_VPRINTF there.
Is there a way to insert #include directives automatically into the verilated code? Or did you have in mind some other way to override these functions?

```
//=========================================================================
// Functions overridable by user defines

#ifndef VL_PRINTF
1. define VL_PRINTF printf	///< Print ala printf; may redefine if desired
#endif
#ifndef VL_VPRINTF
1. define VL_VPRINTF vprintf	///< Print ala vprintf; may redefine if desired
#endif

```

Thanks,
Amir
code_blocks:
//=========================================================================
// Functions overridable by user defines

#ifndef VL_PRINTF
1. define VL_PRINTF printf	///< Print ala printf; may redefine if desired
#endif
#ifndef VL_VPRINTF
1. define VL_VPRINTF vprintf	///< Print ala vprintf; may redefine if desired
#endif
